name: Maple-Wlanut Pound Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup cold unsalted butter or margarine, cut into small cubes
1 1/2 cups unsalted butter or margarine, softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
2. Lightly grease an 18-cup tube pan (angel food pan).
3. For the streusel: In a medium bowl, stir together brown sugar, flour, butter, and cinnamon with a fork or rub mixture with fingertips until crumbly. Stir in walnuts.
4. For the poundcake: With an electric mixer on high speed, beat butter, pancake syrup, brown sugar, vanilla, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl until blended (mixture will look curdled). Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each (mixture still looks curdled).
5. With the mixer on low speed, gradually add flour, beating just until blended and smooth.
6. Spoon half the batter into the prepared pan. Sprinkle with half the streusel.
7. Pour in the remaining batter and spread evenly. Sprinkle the remaining streusel on top.
8. Bake for 1 hour, 15 minutes to 1 hour, 25 minutes, or until a pick inserted in the center comes out with moist crumbs clinging to it.
9. Cool in the pan on a wire rack for 15 minutes. If necessary, run a thin sharp knife around the edge of the pan to loosen the cake.
10. Place a cookie sheet over the pan and carefully invert both. Remove the pan.
11. Place the rack over the cake and turn the cake so the streusel side is up. Cool completely.
12. Store wrapped tightly at room temperature for up to 3 days, or freeze for up to 3 months.
